mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or housing starts and retail sales diverge without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 1.0%, ultra-loose monetary conditions compress equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening labor demand and reduced household income, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales growth signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or equity returns decouple from demand metrics during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation during a supply-constrained regime erodes corporate margins and prompts rate hike expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict direction with accuracy > 53% in walk-forward tests.
